title: Future: will vs going to
explanation:
Talking about the future

Going to — plans and intentions: I'm going to study medicine.

Will — spontaneous decisions and predictions: I'll help you. It will rain.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: I [*am going to visit|'ll visit|visit] my aunt next month.
It's cold. I [*'ll close|am going to close|close] the window.
She [*is going to start|'ll starting|starts] a new job in September.
Don't worry — I [*'ll help|am helping|help] you.
Look at those clouds! It [*is going to rain|'ll rain|rains].
We [*aren't going to buy|won't buying|don't buy] a car this year.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: A: The phone is ringing. B: I ____ it. | *will answer | am going to answer | answer
They ____ married in June. | *are going to get | will getting | get
I think Real Madrid ____ the match. | *will win | is winning | wins
She ____ study abroad next year. | *is going to | will to | goes to

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option to complete the sentences.

questions.text: We [*are going to move|'ll move|move] house soon.
I [*won't be|am not|don't] late, I promise.
He [*is going to learn|'ll learn|learns] to drive.
This bag looks heavy. I [*'ll carry|carry|am carrying] it for you.
They [*are going to have|'ll have|have] a party on Saturday.
